title: xylopicrum acutum
intro: Xylopicrum acutum, also known as the "quassia tree," is a tropical plant native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the family Simaroubaceae. The medicinal properties of Xylopicrum acutum are attributed to its bark, which contains quassin, a compound with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as a natural insecticide. Xylopicrum acutum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including dyspepsia and skin conditions.
